title: Ptycholobium contortum
intro: Ptycholobium contortum, also known as Chrysolaena contorta, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ptycholobium contortum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health issues, as well as to support wound healing and immune system function.
taxonomy: Ptycholobium contortum is classified in the plant kingdom. It is a member of the Fabaceae family, commonly known as legumes.
